item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 'Seared the retinas' is a bit purple, but this is otherwise great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Literally as soon as it opens up

I’ve got it elsewhere

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well it’s possible to be used by humans but they knew human error would occur eventually

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh thats my bad, an oversight on my part.

I will say that the picture of SCP-5200 is where a lot of my confusion stems from. It shows it having switches and knobs, which implies that itd be controlled externally, but it isnt mentioned and the SCP is controlled by -A, who as you mentioned is hooked up through her brain. The object itself is just called a 'device'.

I should mention that I do like everything else in the article, the description of the box itself is the last thing keeping me from a +1 (and I hope that I'm not coming off as rude)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The device is the Brown Box which moves the space object -1, Dana is integrated into it because:

A wired connection between her brain stem and a relay device allowed for all non-anomalous copies of SCP-5200 to send information through that connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My biggest gripe with the article is the description itself; I'm still confused as to what the device actually is, and how -A (and why shes classified as a sub-anomaly if shes just the operator) is using said device. No-vote (for now)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Giant, world-ending space Pong is… really goofy. It's mitigated somewhat by Pong being inspired by the giant, unexplainable thingies up in space, but… It's still space Pong.

ManyMeats hit everything I wanted to say right on the head. This piece is short, climactic, spooky, and funny simultaneously, which is impressive. A worthy 5200, despite its length.

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally, space pong is up. Glad to see this hit the mainlist. And I'm happy you were able to get some more about ʻOumuamua in there. That funky little space rock deserves it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a fun piece with some wide-reaching implications. There's a dose of fridge horror, doses of fun and weird, and just altogether I find this charming. Happy to give it my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who brainstormed this in SCPD with me, to the contest competitors who graciously allowed me to take this spot, and to ManyMeats, DarkStuff, Uncle Nicolini and anyone who else who may be slipping my mind at this moment for making this possible.

I'm beyond thrilled to be bringing you the sequel to The Empty Box. It's called the brown box, and it has been all sorts of fun for me to write.

First image is a cropped version of this one: https://www.flickr.com/photos/chesterbr/9176534752

Other two are pulled from Wikipedia.
